sections.title: Front Matter
sections.body_text:
a For	  comparison,	  the	  TIM-­‐catalyzed	  conversion	  of	  dihydroxyacetone	  phosphate	  to	  glyceraldehyde-­‐3-­‐phosphate has	  been	  assayed	  at $3 0 ^ { \circ } \mathsf { C }$ in $1 0 0 ~ \mathsf { m M }$ triethanolamine-­‐HCl buffer $\mathsf { p } \mathsf { H } \ 7 . 2 7$ ,	   giving	   the	   steady-­‐state	   parameters $k _ { c a t } = 4 3 0 s ^ { - 1 }$ , $K _ { \mathsf { M } } = 0 . 9 7 ~ \mathsf { m M }$ and $k _ { \mathrm { c a t } } / K _ { \mathsf { M } } = 4 . 4 \times { 1 0 } ^ { 5 } { \mathsf { M } } ^ { - 1 } { \mathsf { s } } ^ { - 1 }$ (ref.	  15).	  The $k _ { c a t } / k _ { u n c a t }$ value	  was	  reported	  to	  be $7 . 2 \times$ ${ 1 0 } ^ { 8 }$ but	   because	   the	   published $k _ { \mathrm { u n c a t } }$ value	   [Hall,	   A.	   &	   Knowles,	   J.	   R.,	   Biochemistry 14,	   4348-­‐4352,	   (1975)] overestimates	  the	  uncatalyzed	  rate	  of	  DHAP	  deprotonation $( k _ { \mathsf { H O - } } = 0 . 5 6 \ \mathsf { M } ^ { - 1 } \ s ^ { - 1 }$ , ref.	   10), $k _ { c a t } / ( k _ { \mathsf { H O - } } [ \mathsf { H O } ] ) = 4 . 1 \ \mathsf { x }$ ${ 1 0 } ^ { 9 }$ gives	  a	  more	  realistic	  estimate	  of	  the	  enzyme’s	  catalytic	  prowess.

b The	  advantage	  over	  acetate	  in	  solution	  was	  calculated	  using	  the	  second-­‐order	  rate	  constant	  for	  the	  acetate-­‐ promoted	   reaction	   measured	   at	   pH	   7	   and $2 7 ^ { \circ } \mathsf { C }$ in	   the	   presence	   of $10 \%$ MeOH $( k _ { \mathsf { A c O - } } = 5 . 7 7 \times 1 0 ^ { - 5 } \mathsf { M } ^ { - 1 } \mathsf { s } ^ { - 1 } )$ .	   For comparison,	  the	  rate	  constant	  for	  hydroxide-­‐catalyzed	  deprotonation of	  5-­‐nitrobenzisoxazole is $1 5 \boldsymbol { \mathsf { M } } ^ { - 1 } \boldsymbol { \mathsf { S } } ^ { - 1 }$ (ref. 9).

c Rate	   accelerations	   for	   the	   Kemp	   eliminases	   were	   calculated	   based	   on	   the	   rate	   constant	   for	   the	   uncatalyzed reaction	  determined	  in	  ref.	  12 $( k _ { u n c a t } = 1 . 1 6 \times { 1 0 } ^ { - 6 } { { s } } ^ { - 1 } )$ .

d Assay	  conditions: $2 0 ^ { \circ } \mathsf { C } ,$ $4 0 \mathsf { m } \mathsf { M }$ sodium	  phosphate	  pH	  7.4,	  100	  mM	  NaCl.

e Assay	  conditions: $2 7 ^ { \circ } \mathsf { C }$ ,	  25	  mM	  HEPES	  pH	  7.25,	  100	  mM	  NaCl, $1 . 5 \%$ MeCN, $1 . 2 5 \%$ glycero f Although	   5-­‐nitrobenzisoxazole	   was	   used	   during	   optimization	   of	   KE59,	   the	   best	   substrate	   for	   KE59.13	   was found	   to	   be	   5,7-­‐dichlorobenzisoxazole: $k _ { c a t } = 2 1 \ s ^ { - 1 }$ , $K _ { \mathsf { M } } = 0 . 0 3 7 \ \mathsf { m M }$ and $k _ { \mathrm { c a t } } / K _ { \mathsf { M } } = 5 . 7 \times 1 0 ^ { 5 } \mathrm { M } ^ { - 1 } \mathsf { s } ^ { - 1 }$ .	   	   The	   rate acceleration $( k _ { c a t } / k _ { u n c a t } )$ for	  this	  substrate	  is $2 . 5 \times { 1 0 } ^ { 7 }$ (ref.	  14).

g Assay	   conditions: $2 7 ^ { \circ } \mathsf C ,$ , $5 0 ~ \mathsf { m M }$ sodium	   phosphate	   buffer $\mathsf { p H } ~ 7 . 0$ ,	   100	   mM	   NaCl, $10 \%$ MeOH.	   Errors correspond	  to	  standard	  deviations	  determined	  from	  at	  least	  two	  independent	  measurements.	   For	  HG3.17,	  six measurements	  on three	  independent	  protein	  batches were	  made.	  	  For	  comparison,	  the $k _ { \mathrm { c a t } }$ and $K _ { \mathfrak { m } }$ values	  for the	  starting	  HG3	  design	  were	  reported	  to	  be $1 . 7 \pm 0 . 1 { \ s } ^ { - 1 }$ and $2 . 4 \pm 0 . 2 { \mathrm { ~ m M } }$ in	   the	   same	   buffer	   but	   containing $2 \%$ acetonitrile	  instead	  of $10 \%$ methanol	  (ref.	  3).

h	  	  Assay	  conditions: $2 7 ^ { \circ } \mathsf { C }$ , $5 0 ~ { \mathsf { m M } }$ sodium	  phosphate	  buffer	  pH	  7.0,	  100	  mM	  NaCl, $2 \%$ MeCN

sections.title: HG3:
sections.body_text:


HG3.3b: V6I, K50H,	  M84C, S89R,	  Q90D, A125N



HG3.7: V6I,	  Q37K,	  K50Q,	  M84C,	  S89R,	  Q90H,	  A125N



HG3.14: V6I,	  Q37K,	  K50Q,	  G82A,	  M84C,	  Q90H,	  T105I,	  A125T,	  T142N,	  T208M, T279S, D300N



HG3.17: V6I,	  Q37K,	  N47E,	  K50Q,	  G82A,	  M84C,	  S89N,	  Q90F,	  T105I,	  A125T,	  T142N,	  T208M,	  F267M, W275A,	  R276F,	  T279S,	  D300N



Supplementary Figure	  2 |	  Amino	  acid	  sequences	  of	  representative	  HG3	  variants.	  Sequence numbering of	   HG3 is	   based	   on	   the	   X-­‐ray	   structure	   of	   HG2 (PDB code	   3NYQ,	   chain	   A), the first	   two residues of	   which are	   not	   resolved.	   The	   HG3	   sequence	   is	   used	   as	   reference	   for	   the	   highlighted mutations	   (orange $\underline { { \underline { { \mathbf { \delta \pi } } } } }$ residue	   was	   computationally	   designed into	   the	   TAX	   xylanase	   scaffold	   1GOR, green $\underline { { \underline { { \mathbf { \delta \pi } } } } }$ residue	   was	   mutated	   once	   in	   the	   course	   of	   evolution,	   cyan $\underline { { \underline { { \mathbf { \delta \pi } } } } }$ residue	   was	   mutated	   twice	   in the	  course	  of	  evolution,	  magenta $\underline { { \underline { { \mathbf { \delta \pi } } } } }$ residue	  was	  mutated	  three	  times	  in	  the	  course	  of	  evolution).

HG3. (a)	  Thermal	  denaturation	  of	  HG3.17	  (red)	  and	  HG3	  (blue).	  (b)	  pH-­‐rate	  profiles	  for	  HG3.17	  (red) and	   HG3	   (blue).	   (c)	   Cleavage	   of $5 0 \mu \mathsf { M }$ 5-­‐nitrobenzisoxazole	   by	   HG3.17	   variants	   (background	   buffer reaction:	   orange;	   D127A:	   green $( [ { \mathsf { H G 3 . 1 7 ~ D 1 2 7 A } } ] = 1 0 ~ { \mu \mathsf { M } } )$ ;	   D127N:	   purple $( [ \mathsf { H G 3 . 1 7 ~ D 1 2 7 N } ] = 1 0$ $\mu \mathsf { M } )$ );	  Q50A:	  black $( [ { \mathsf { H G 3 . 1 7 } } \mathsf { Q 5 0 A } ] = 1 0 0 \mathsf { n M } )$ );	  HG3.17	  (wt):	  red $( [ { \mathsf { H G 3 . 1 7 } } ] = 1 0 0 \mathsf { n M } _ { \mathrm { \Omega } }$ )).	  For	  HG3.17	  and HG3.17	   Q50A,	   data	   points	   are	   shown	   at	   1	   s	   intervals.	   For	   the	   other	   measurements,	   points	   are	   only shown	   at	   33	   s intervals	   for	   clarity.	   (d) $K _ { \mathrm { i } }$ determination	   of	   the	   transition	   state	   analog	   6-­‐ nitrobenzotriazole	  with	  HG3.17	  (red, $K _ { \mathrm { i } } = 2 ~ \mu \mathrm { M }$ )	   and	   HG3	   (blue, $K _ { \mathrm { i } } = 3 1 0 ~ \mu \mathrm { M } _ { \mathrm { \ell } } ^ { \prime }$ )	   at $\mathsf { p H } 6 . 0$ .	   (e)	   Brønsted plot	   for	   cleavage	   of	   substituted	   benzisoxazoles	   reveals	   the	   discriminating	   nature	   of	   HG3.17.	   The $\beta$ value	  for	  the	  5-­‐substituted	  substrates	  is	  -­‐1.36.	  	  (f)	  The	  length	  and	  width	  of	  the	  substrate	  entry	  tunnel of	  HG3.17	  (red)	  and	  HG2 $\stackrel { \cdot } { = }$ HG3-­‐T265S,	  blue)	  were	  measured	  with	  the	  PyMOL	  plugin	  Caver	  3.0	  (ref. 47). Since	   the	   structure	   of	   HG2	   was	   solved	   without	   hydrogen	   atoms,	   these	   were	   added	   in	   PyMOL before	  performing	  the	  calculation.

given	   as	   the	   difference	   between	   the	   optimal	   angles	   calculated	   for	   hydrogen	   bonding	   interactions between	   acetamide	   dimers47 $( \Psi = 1 1 2 . 3 ^ { \circ }$ , $\theta = 1 5 9 . 4 ^ { \circ }$ , $\mathsf { X } = 1 7 7 . 5 ^ { \circ }$ )	   and	   the	   measured	   values	   for	   the hydrogen	  bond	  between	  6-­‐nitrobenzotriazole	  and	  the	  catalytic	  base	  Asp127 in	  HG2 (blue, $\Psi = 1 3 4 . 7 ^ { \circ }$ , $\theta = 1 7 2 . 1 ^ { \circ }$ , $\mathsf { X } = 1 5 6 . 4 ^ { \circ }$ )	   and	   HG3.17	   (red, $\Psi = 1 2 1 . 1 ^ { \circ }$ , $\theta = 1 5 5 . 5 ^ { \circ }$ , $\mathsf { X } = 1 6 7 . 3 ^ { \circ }$ ). For	   the	   HG2 (i.e.	   HG3-­‐ T265S)	  structure3 ,	  the	  transition	  state	  analog	  orientation	  most	  closely	  resembling	  that	  in	  HG3.17	  was used.	   In	   order	   to	   model the	   position	   of	   the	   acidic proton	   at	   N1 in	   the	   HG2	   complex,	   a	   calculated structure	  of	  6-­‐nitrobenzotriazole	  was	  pair-­‐fitted	  onto	  the	  ligand in	  the	  X-­‐ray	  structure. $\Psi$ is	  the	  angle at	  the	  acceptor	  atom;	  θ,	  the	  angle	  at	  the	  hydrogen	  atom;	  and $\scriptstyle x ,$ the	  torsional	  angle	  around	  the $\mathtt { C - O ^ { - } }$ axis.
